origin.name: Liberia
origin.death_registration: Center for National Documents and Records Agency
origin.key_document: Death certificate from Center for National Documents and Records Agency (English language)
origin.source: FCDO Liberia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Monrovia guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Liberia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
special_rules: Liberia issues death certificates in English, which simplifies UK documentation requirements.
Police clearance from the Liberia National Police is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from Liberian authorities is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. Liberian documentation is in English. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Monrovia can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Liberia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Liberia. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. National Records Agency, police clearance, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
